spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates dictionary-vs-no-dictionary compression comparison but the savings number is computed by JS arithmetic on textarea lengths, not by a real shared-brotli encoder.

interactive.rationale: Editable dictionary and content textareas drive metrics. Real interactive surface even if simulated.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page does not run real brotli/zstd; the simulation needs a disclaimer.

edge_cases.rationale: Doesn't model dictionary-cache miss, SHA-256 integrity check, dictionary version mismatch.

openQuestions.title: Wire a real CompressionStream or WASM brotli encoder
openQuestions.detail: Right now the savings metric is JS arithmetic. Bundle brotli-wasm or call a server route that brotli-encodes against a fixed dictionary so the displayed savings ratio is real.

openQuestions.title: Add a SHA-256 mismatch scenario
openQuestions.detail: CDT requires Available-Dictionary's sha256 to match the cache; show what happens when it doesn't.

openQuestions.title: Add a 'simulated' disclaimer
openQuestions.detail: A yellow warn-block at top noting the numbers are simulated so visitors don't take savings ratios as real measurements.

summary: Visually convincing but never runs a real shared-brotli encoder. The savings metric is JS arithmetic on textarea lengths.
